key_drivers: US-Iran War: Supply disruption of ~20% global crude/gas, WTI Apr contract at $90.90, oil approaching $100/bbl
Risk-Off Spike: VIX surged to 29.49 (+24% on March 6), equities falling, safe havens bid
BoJ Hawkish Stance: Rate at 1.0%, Governor Ueda vowing continued hikes, JPY strengthening on safe haven + rate divergence
China PMI Contraction: Official manufacturing PMI at 49.0 (4-month low, below 50), pressuring AUD and commodity currencies
Gold ETF Outflows: GLD saw $2.91B outflow on March 5 (largest in decade), yet spot gold resilient near $5,100-5,175

fallbacks_used: CNBC US10YTIP for real-time 10Y TIPS (1.788% March 6 close, eased from 1.82% on March 5)
TradingView TVC:DXY for USD index (98.855, -0.21% March 6)
EIA highlights PDF for inventory data (+3.5M barrels week ending Feb 27)
Barchart/Yahoo Finance for WTI crude (CLJ26 $90.90 Apr contract)
overnight_changes: VIX spiked +24% to 29.49 on March 6 (geopolitical shock from Iran war widening)
WTI crude surged to ~$90.90 (Apr contract) on Iran supply disruption, Goldman warns $100 within days
Gold near $5,100-5,175/oz on safe haven demand despite record GLD ETF outflows ($2.91B on March 5)
10Y TIPS yield eased to 1.788% on March 6 (down from 1.82% on March 5) - slight relief
MOVE index at ~74.53 (elevated but below recent high of 81.26)

imminent: EIA Weekly Petroleum Report March 11, 2026 - critical for CL direction
US-Iran War escalation/de-escalation risk (ongoing) - Strait of Hormuz threats

BoJ March policy meeting decision (likely hold due to geopolitical volatility)

background: BoJ hawkish tightening cycle continues (rate at 1.0%, vow to raise further)
RBA hiking cycle resumed (3.85% after Feb 3 hike, first in 2+ years)
